title: Golden
description:
There are many special irrational numbers in nature. One is the “golden ratio” represented by the Greek letter phi, φ, which has a value of

φ = (1 + √5) / 2 = 1.61803399.....

Given 2 real numbers, determine whether their ratio is equal (or close to) the golden ratio. For this program, use ±1% as the tolerance.
input: The first line will contain the number of data sets. Each data set consists of 2 real numbers (with or without decimals).
output: Either print out “golden” or “not” for each data set.
source: School, PLU High School Programming Contest, PLU 2014, Advanced
